name: Eiffel Tower
description: The iconic wrought-iron lattice tower on the Champ de Mars in Paris, France.

name: Great Wall of China
description: A series of fortifications built across the historical northern borders of China, spanning thousands of miles.

name: Machu Picchu
description: An ancient 15th-century Incan citadel set high in the Andes Mountains in Peru, famous for its dry-stone walls.

name: Taj Mahal
description: An ivory-white marble mausoleum on the south bank of the Yamuna river in the Indian city of Agra, commissioned in 1632.

name: The Colosseum
description: The largest ancient amphitheatre ever built, located in the centre of the city of Rome, Italy.

name: Pyramids of Giza
description: The complex includes the Great Pyramid of Giza, the Pyramid of Khafre, and the Pyramid of Menkaure, bordering Giza in Greater Cairo.

name: Statue of Liberty
description: A colossal neoclassical sculpture on Liberty Island in New York Harbor, USA, a symbol of freedom and democracy.

name: Bora Bora
description: A small South Pacific island northwest of Tahiti, known for its stunning turquoise lagoons and overwater bungalows.

name: Petra
description: A famous archaeological site in Jordan, known for its rock-cut architecture, particularly The Treasury (Al-Khazneh).

name: Acropolis of Athens
description: An ancient citadel located on a rocky outcrop above Athens, home to the Parthenon, dedicated to the goddess Athena.

name: Angkor Wat
description: A temple complex in Cambodia and the largest religious monument in the world, originally Hindu, then Buddhist.

name: Rio de Janeiro (Christ the Redeemer)
description: A 30-meter Art Deco statue of Jesus Christ on top of Corcovado mountain, overlooking Rio de Janeiro.

name: Sydney Opera House
description: A multi-venue performing arts centre in Sydney, Australia, defined by its iconic 'sail' structures.

name: The Alps
description: The highest and most extensive mountain range system in Europe, famous for mountaineering and picturesque valleys.

name: Kyoto (Fushimi Inari-taisha)
description: Known for the thousands of vibrant red torii gates that wind through the forest up the mountain, dedicated to the Shinto rice god Inari.

name: Victoria Falls
description: Locally known as Mosi-oa-Tunya ('The Smoke that Thunders'), it's a massive waterfall on the Zambezi River.

name: Rajgir
description: Rajgir is an ancient city in the northeast Indian state of Bihar. Surrounded by hills, it was the first capital of the Magadha kingdom.

name: Patratu Valley
description: A stunning valley and dam area in Jharkhand, India, popular for its winding ghat road with panoramic views.

name: Santorini
description: A volcanic Greek island famous for its white-washed villages, blue-domed churches, and spectacular sunsets over the Aegean Sea.

name: Halong Bay
description: A UNESCO World Heritage Site in Vietnam, known for its thousands of towering limestone islands and calm waters.
